concept_name: Magnetisation & Magnetic Intensity — B = μ₀(H + M)
source_book: NCERT Class 12 Ch.5 Magnetism and Matter §5.5 (Magnetisation and magnetic intensity) — table-of-contents reference only; teaching authored from first principles. The atomic concept: a solenoid supplies the magnetic INTENSITY H = nI (set by the free current, independent of any material); a material core develops a MAGNETISATION M (net magnetic dipole moment per unit volume) as its atomic dipoles align to H; the total field inside is B = μ₀(H + M). The susceptibility χ = M/H (and relative permeability μ_r = 1 + χ) classifies matter into diamagnetic (χ < 0), paramagnetic (small χ > 0) and ferromagnetic (large χ). Built instrument-native in the straightforward (non-Socratic) model: five motion-distinct teaching beats + one combined interactive state.

real_world_anchor.primary: Picture an electromagnet in a scrapyard crane, or the iron core inside any transformer. The coil carries a current and produces the applied field H. But the lift is thousands of times stronger than the bare coil would give — because the iron core magnetises: its atomic dipoles line up, adding their own magnetisation M, so the real field is B = μ₀(H + M). That is why every powerful electromagnet has an iron core.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Picture winding a coil around a plain plastic rod versus an iron nail. Same current, same H — but the iron nail becomes a strong magnet and the plastic does almost nothing. The difference is the material's susceptibility χ: how much magnetisation M each unit of H produces.

n.name: Turns per unit length of the solenoid

formulas.magnetic_intensity: H = n I  (set by the free current; units A/m)
formulas.magnetisation: M = χ H  (the material's response; units A/m)
formulas.permeability: μ_r = 1 + χ,  so  B = μ₀ μ_r H
formulas.susceptibility: χ = M / H  (dia: χ < 0, para: small χ > 0, ferro: large χ)

constraints: H is the applied magnetic intensity, fixed by the free current in the solenoid (H = nI) and independent of the core material
M is the magnetisation of the material — net magnetic dipole moment per unit volume — produced when its atomic dipoles align to H
Inside the material the TOTAL field is B = μ₀(H + M), not just μ₀H
Susceptibility χ = M/H: diamagnetic χ slightly negative (dipoles oppose), paramagnetic χ small positive, ferromagnetic χ very large positive
Relative permeability μ_r = 1 + χ; a ferromagnetic core multiplies the field by μ_r (hundreds to thousands)
H and M share the same unit A/m; B is in tesla

aha_moment.statement: The applied field H aligns the atomic dipoles, so the rod becomes magnetised.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: As the current rises, the jumbled atomic arrows swing into alignment and the net M arrow grows from zero.

STATE_1.title: Apply the field: magnetic intensity H

scene_composition.text: H = n I  (the field you apply)

tts_sentences.text_en: Start with a solenoid carrying a current. The current sets up a magnetic field along the axis — we call its strength the magnetic intensity H.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक solenoid से शुरू करें जिसमें current बह रही है। यह current axis के साथ एक magnetic field बनाती है — इसकी strength को हम magnetic intensity H कहते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: current pravahistunna oka solenoid tho modalupettandi. ee current axis venta oka magnetic field ni erpaduthundi — dani strength ni manam magnetic intensity H ani pilustamu.

tts_sentences.text_en: H is set only by the free current: H equals n times I, where n is the turns per metre. More current, more H.
tts_sentences.text_hi: H sirf free current से तय होती है: H equals n into I, जहाँ n है turns per metre। ज़्यादा current, ज़्यादा H।
tts_sentences.text_te: H kevalam free current tho matrame set avutundi: H equals n into I, ikkada n ante turns per metre. ekkuva current, ekkuva H.

tts_sentences.text_en: With nothing inside, the field is just B-naught equals mu-naught times H. Now watch what happens when we put matter in.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अंदर कुछ न हो तो field सिर्फ B-naught equals mu-naught into H होती है। अब देखिए जब हम अंदर matter डालते हैं तो क्या होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: lopala emi lekapote field kevalam B-naught equals mu-naught into H matrame. ippudu manam lopala matter pettinappudu emi jarugutundo choodandi.

misconception_watch.belief: The magnetic intensity H depends on what material you put inside the coil
misconception_watch.visual_counter: H comes from the coil current alone (H = nI); the internal field lines are the same before any core is inserted.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: H is fixed by the free current, H = nI, and does not depend on the core material.

scene_composition.text: slide a rod into the coil

tts_sentences.text_en: Slide a material rod into the solenoid. Every material is built from countless atomic magnets — tiny magnetic dipoles.
tts_sentences.text_hi: solenoid के अंदर एक material rod डालिए। हर material countless atomic magnets से बना है — tiny magnetic dipoles।
tts_sentences.text_te: solenoid lopaliki oka material rod ni jarapandi. prati material lekkaleni atomic magnets tho nirmimchabadindi — tiny magnetic dipoles.

tts_sentences.text_en: On their own these atomic dipoles point every which way, jumbled and random. Their arrows cancel out.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अपने आप ये atomic dipoles हर दिशा में point करते हैं, jumbled और random। इनके arrows एक दूसरे को cancel कर देते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: vaatantata ee atomic dipoles prati diksha lo point chestayi, jumbled mariyu random. vaati arrows okadaanini okati cancel chestayi.

tts_sentences.text_en: So the material starts with essentially no net magnetisation — M is nearly zero. Nothing is aligned yet.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तो material की शुरुआत में essentially कोई net magnetisation नहीं होती — M लगभग zero है। अभी कुछ भी aligned नहीं है।
tts_sentences.text_te: kaabatti material modatlo essentially net magnetisation emi undadu — M daadaapu zero. inkaa emi align avvaledu.

misconception_watch.belief: Every material is already magnetic before any field is applied
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The inserted rod's atomic arrows point randomly and its net M reads zero until H acts on them.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Ordinary matter has randomly-oriented atomic dipoles, so net M is zero until an applied H aligns them.

STATE_3.title: H aligns them: magnetisation M appears

scene_composition.text: H lines the dipoles up → M grows

M = magnetic moment
per unit volume

more current → more H
→ stronger alignment

tts_sentences.text_en: Now turn the current up so the intensity H grows. Watch the atomic dipoles swing around and line up with the field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब current बढ़ाइए ताकि intensity H बढ़े। देखिए atomic dipoles घूमकर field के साथ line up हो जाते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ippudu current perigandi, intensity H perigela. atomic dipoles tirigi field tho line up ayye vidhanam choodandi.

tts_sentences.text_en: As they align, their little arrows add up instead of cancelling. The rod develops a net magnetisation M.
tts_sentences.text_hi: जैसे-जैसे वे align होते हैं, उनके little arrows cancel होने के बजाय add up होते हैं। rod में एक net magnetisation M develop होती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: avi align ayye kolla, vaati little arrows cancel avvakunda add up avutayi. rod lo oka net magnetisation M develop avutundi.

tts_sentences.text_en: That is magnetisation: the net magnetic moment per unit volume of the material. The applied field created it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यही magnetisation है: material के per unit volume net magnetic moment। applied field ने इसे बनाया।
tts_sentences.text_te: ade magnetisation: material yokka per unit volume net magnetic moment. applied field dinni srustimchindi.

misconception_watch.belief: Magnetisation M is just another name for the applied field H
misconception_watch.visual_counter: H is the coil's field (green lines); M is the material's own response — the red arrow that grows only as the atomic dipoles align.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: H is the applied intensity; M is the material's induced magnetisation — two different things, linked by M = χH.

STATE_4.title: The total field: B = μ₀(H + M)

scene_composition.text:
field lines pack denser
inside the core

applied H + material M
= total field B

tts_sentences.text_en: Inside the magnetised rod the field is no longer just the applied part. The material's magnetisation M adds to H.
tts_sentences.text_hi: magnetised rod के अंदर field अब सिर्फ applied part नहीं है। material की magnetisation M, H में add होती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: magnetised rod lopala field ippudu kevalam applied part kaadu. material yokka magnetisation M, H ki add avutundi.

tts_sentences.text_en: The total field is B equals mu-naught times the sum H plus M. Watch the internal field lines pack in denser.
tts_sentences.text_hi: total field है B equals mu-naught into sum H plus M। देखिए internal field lines और denser पैक होती हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: total field ante B equals mu-naught into sum H plus M. internal field lines inka denser ga pack ayye vidhanam choodandi.

tts_sentences.text_en: The meter climbs: the same current now gives a much stronger B, because the core is doing part of the work.
tts_sentences.text_hi: meter बढ़ता है: वही current अब बहुत stronger B देती है, क्योंकि core काम का एक हिस्सा कर रहा है।
tts_sentences.text_te: meter perugutundi: ade current ippudu chaala stronger B istundi, endukante core pani lo kొంత bhaagam chestondi.

misconception_watch.belief: The field inside a material core is just mu-naught times H, the same as empty space
misconception_watch.visual_counter: When the core magnetises, the internal field lines pack denser and the B meter jumps above mu-naught H.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Inside matter B = μ₀(H + M), which exceeds μ₀H whenever the core magnetises (M > 0).

STATE_5.title: Three kinds of matter: χ and μ_r

tts_sentences.text_en: How much magnetisation you get per unit of intensity is the susceptibility: M equals chi times H.
tts_sentences.text_hi: per unit intensity कितनी magnetisation मिलती है वही susceptibility है: M equals chi into H।
tts_sentences.text_te: per unit intensity ki entha magnetisation vastundo ade susceptibility: M equals chi into H.

tts_sentences.text_en: Swap the material and watch. Diamagnetic: chi is slightly negative, the dipoles weakly oppose. Paramagnetic: a small positive chi, weak alignment.
tts_sentences.text_hi: material बदलिए और देखिए। Diamagnetic: chi थोड़ी negative, dipoles weakly oppose करते हैं। Paramagnetic: छोटी positive chi, weak alignment।
tts_sentences.text_te: material maarchandi mariyu choodandi. Diamagnetic: chi konchem negative, dipoles weakly oppose chestayi. Paramagnetic: chinna positive chi, weak alignment.

tts_sentences.text_en: Ferromagnetic: chi is enormous, the dipoles snap into full alignment and B is multiplied by mu-r equals one plus chi — hundreds of times stronger.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Ferromagnetic: chi बहुत बड़ी, dipoles full alignment में snap होते हैं और B, mu-r equals one plus chi से गुणा होती है — hundreds of times stronger।
tts_sentences.text_te: Ferromagnetic: chi chaala peddadi, dipoles full alignment loki snap avutayi mariyu B, mu-r equals one plus chi tho guninchabadutundi — hundreds of times stronger.

misconception_watch.belief: All materials respond to a magnetic field the same way
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Cycling dia, para and ferro shows opposite/weak/violent alignment and a mu_r readout from below 1 to hundreds.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Susceptibility χ differs by material: dia (χ<0), para (small χ>0), ferro (huge χ) — so μ_r = 1+χ ranges from below 1 to hundreds.

scene_composition.text: Drag the current · pick the material

bigger current → bigger H → more M
pick ferro → B jumps
H = nI  ·  M = χH  ·  B = μ₀(H+M)

tts_sentences.text_en: Your turn. Drag the current to change the applied intensity H, and pick the material to change its susceptibility chi.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आपकी बारी। current खींचकर applied intensity H बदलिए, और material चुनकर उसकी susceptibility chi बदलिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ippudu mee vantu. current lagi applied intensity H maarchandi, mariyu material enchi daani susceptibility chi maarchandi.

tts_sentences.text_en: Bigger current gives a bigger H and more magnetisation M. Switch to a ferromagnetic core and the field B jumps hundreds of times.
tts_sentences.text_hi: ज़्यादा current से ज़्यादा H और ज़्यादा magnetisation M। ferromagnetic core पर switch कीजिए और field B hundreds of times बढ़ जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ekkuva current tho ekkuva H mariyu ekkuva magnetisation M. ferromagnetic core ki switch cheyandi mariyu field B hundreds of times perugutundi.

tts_sentences.text_en: One rule ties it all together: H equals nI, M equals chi H, and B equals mu-naught times H plus M. That is magnetisation and magnetic intensity.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक rule सब कुछ जोड़ता है: H equals nI, M equals chi H, और B equals mu-naught into H plus M। यही है magnetisation और magnetic intensity।
tts_sentences.text_te: oka rule anni kalipi unchutundi: H equals nI, M equals chi H, mariyu B equals mu-naught into H plus M. ade magnetisation mariyu magnetic intensity.

STATE_1.label: Apply the field: intensity H = nI
STATE_1.caption: Current in the coil sets the magnetic intensity H = nI

STATE_2.label: Insert matter: jumbled atomic dipoles
STATE_2.caption: Atomic dipoles point randomly → net M ≈ 0

STATE_3.label: H aligns them: M appears
STATE_3.caption: Raise the current → dipoles align → M grows

STATE_4.label: Total field: B = μ₀(H + M)
STATE_4.caption: M adds to H → field lines densify → B climbs

STATE_5.label: Three kinds of matter: χ and μ_r
STATE_5.caption: dia / para / ferro cycle — M = χH, μ_r = 1+χ

STATE_6.label: Explore: current + material
STATE_6.caption: Drag current · pick material — watch H, M, B, μ_r
STATE_6.formula_overlay: H = nI  ·  M = χH  ·  B = μ₀(H+M)
